Off Calendar Sep-17-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Benjamin Martin, Esq., (415-558-1760) for Palintiff/Opposing party Phillip Garcia
Katelyn Knight, Esq., (415-524-0477) of Murchison & Cumming LLP for Dfendnat-Moving party.

Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral arguments adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT CARRIE WILSON and ANGELO WILSON'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is overruled as to the 2nd and 5th causes of action. Common law and constitutional invasion of privacy are sufficiently alleged. Demurrer is overruled as to the 7th cause of action for enforcement of debt. Res judicata is not applicable under the facts of this case. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the 3rd and 4th causes of action. There is no private right of action under Penal Code 647 and moving defendant is not alleged to be the perpetrator per CCP 632/637.2. Plaintiff's request for leave to amend to allege a new cause of action under CC 1708.8 is denied without prejudice as not properly before the Court on this motion. The defendant The wilson Family Trust has 10 days to file an answer from date of entry of order. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Pursuant To Ccp 425.16
Off Calendar Nov-19-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Katelyn M. Knight, Esq. of Murchison & Cumming LLP, (415-524-4300), for Angelo Wilson, Defendant and moving party.
Benjamin Martin, Esq., (415-558-1760), for Phillip Garcia, Plaintiff and opposing party.

Defendant submits to the ruling denying its motion. Court hears arguments on the request for sanctions by plaintiff.

Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral arguments rules as follows: DEFENDANT ANGELO WILSON'S Motion To Strike Pursuant To CCP 425.16 is DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain burden and show that Plaintiff's causes of action arise out of protected activity. As to the 8th cause of action (wrongful eviction), as alleged, it is based on failure to address noise complaints; filming and audio recording Plaintiff's residence; and refusal to cash rent checks. As to the "claim based on Defendant's failure to cash his rent checks" (Notice of Motion 1:25-26), Defendant cites no authority for the proposition that a single claim within a cause of action based on multiple grounds may be stricken pursuant to CCP 425.16.

Plaintiff's request for sanctions is taken under submission. Plaintiff's counsel submits proposed order with no amount on sanctions as instructed by Court. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.
